file_name: 8 April 2026_How the structure of online reviews shapes their helpfulness.md
paper_title: The role of review structure in perceived helpfulness
insight_1: For highly rated products, reviews that start with criticisms before shifting to positive evaluations are more credible and balanced.
insight_2: For average-rated products, escalating negativity in the review is most helpful for readers who evaluate products of middling quality.
insight_3: For low-rated products, positive openings make reviews more helpful. Reviews that begin positively and remain steady in tone are perceived as more receptive by recipients.
insight_4: Micro-prompts on review platforms can be structured to encourage reviewers to start with minor issues for highly rated products or to open constructively for low-rated products.

paper_title: Prohibition of 'dark patterns' in online financial services contracts
insight_1: The new Art. 246b § 4 Introductory Act to the Civil Code (EGBGB) will prohibit the use of manipulative practices ('dark patterns') when concluding distance contracts for financial services.
insight_2: Specifically, companies must design their online user interfaces in such a way that consumers are not deceived or manipulated or otherwise materially distorted or impaired in their ability to make a free and informed decision when concluding an online financial services contract.
insight_3: Violations of the new provisions may also constitute misleading commercial practices under the new Sec. 5(6) UWG, leading to claims under the UWG (in particular injunctive relief, removal, and damages).
insight_4: Financial service providers are advised to check their websites and apps to see whether and what adjustments they need to make in order to comply with the requirements on dark patterns from 19 June 2026.

file_name: 17 December 2025_A quantum computing leap for financial services-.md
paper_title: A quantum computing leap for financial services
insight_1: Explainability is central to fair outcomes and regulatory confidence.
insight_2: Market consolidation in the quantum ecosystem may lead to risks of concentrations and dependency.
insight_3: Quantum methods can speed up Monte Carlo estimates by reducing error reduction requirements.
insight_4: There are significant hardware, software, integration, algorithmic, and cost challenges for applying quantum computing to financial services.

file_name: 17 December 2025_EU Markets Integration & Supervision Package- Key Proposals.md
paper_title: EU Markets Integration & Supervision Package: Key Proposals
insight_1: The European Commission published the Market Integration and Supervision Package to achieve targeted amendments to existing financial services legislation.
insight_2: The SIU, a key pillar of the EU Commission's strategy to deepen capital markets integration, is part of the Market Integration and Supervision Package.
insight_3: The existing fragmentation within the EU Single Market has erected intra-EU barriers hindering cross-border provision of services equivalent to a tariff rate of 110% (on services alone).
insight_4: The Commission believes that one of the main reasons for the high market capitalisation in financial markets is due to a very high level of fragmentation of the EU Single Market for financial services.

insight_1: Design of the cancellation page, including elements permitted on the confirmation page and prohibition of dark patterns.
insight_2: Authentication requirements for cancellation requests, including excessive authentication and password request limitations.
insight_3: Scope of the law under scrutiny regarding the term 'website' for cancellation button requirements.
insight_4: Upcoming EU-wide withdrawal function implementation in Germany.

file_name: 20 January 2026_Fintech Outlook 2026- Key trends to watch.md
paper_title: 20 January 2026 Fintech Outlook - Key trends to watch
insight_1: EMIs will become a sub-category of payment institutions (PIs) under the new framework.
insight_2: The PSR will contain conduct requirements applicable to payment institutions and e-money issuers, including transparency and information requirements as well as provisions on rights and obligations of users.
insight_3: Requirements on strong customer authentication (SCA) will be contained in the PSR from now on.
insight_4: The revised framework introduces stricter fraud prevention requirements for payment institutions and imposes new obligations on online platform operators.

file_name: 20 March 2026_Limits to the GDPR Right of Access and the ECJ’s New Causality Brake.md
paper_title: Limits to the GDPR Right of Access and the ECJ’s New Causality Brake
insight_1: The ECJ has introduced a substantial limitation through the plea of abuse of rights for the right of access under Art. 15 GDPR.
insight_2: The ECJ confirms that even an initial request for access can be 'excessive' within the meaning of Art. 12(5) GDPR, based on the abusive intent rather than the mere number of requests.
insight_3: The ECJ's ruling on Article 82(1) of the GDPR clarifies that a claim for damages can arise from infringements not involving direct data processing, such as the refusal to provide access.
insight_4: The ECJ establishes that the causal link can be broken through own conduct, allowing controllers to defend themselves by proving the data subject's own conduct was the decisive cause of any damage claimed.

file_name: 21 April 2026_Online coaching and distance learning- Federal Court of Justice clarifies application of the Distance Learning Protection Act.md
paper_title: Federal Court of Justice clarifies application of the Distance Learning Protection Act
insight_1: Synchronous online teaching may be equivalent to face-to-face teaching and thus fall outside the scope of the FernUSG.
insight_2: Monitoring of learning success is deemed to exist even where participants have a contractual right to ask questions, as long as they can test their understanding of the learning material through live coaching sessions.
insight_3: The Federal Court of Justice has clarified that synchronous online teaching involving 'bidirectional real-time communication' differs fundamentally from distance learning methods where participants study independently.
insight_4: There is strong evidence to suggest that the application of the FernUSG should be rejected if the synchronous components predominate (> 50%).

file_name: 22 April 2026_#7- State of the EU Crypto Industry- MiCA, Tokenisation and What Comes Next.md
paper_title: State of the EU Crypto Industry: MiCA, Tokenisation and What Comes Next
insight_1: The EU regulatory landscape for crypto-assets is evolving at pace, bringing both clarity and complexity for market participants.
insight_2: This episode unpacks the implications of the MiCA framework, alongside the opportunities and risks associated with stablecoins and their growing role in digital finance.
insight_3: The conversation also explores the tokenization of financial instruments and the expanding relevance of distributed ledger technology (DLT) within existing regulatory structures.
insight_4: Highlighting how innovation is being shaped – and sometimes constrained – by regulation.

file_name: 23 January 2026_EU Inc.- Founders and companies with expansion plans must now think strategically.md
paper_title: EU Inc.: Founders and companies with expansion plans must now think strategically
insight_1: The European Union aims to make it much easier to start a business and strengthen the single market as a unified economic area.
insight_2: Foundations of EU Inc. are to be established as a private limited liability company, allowing founders' personal assets to remain protected.
insight_3: EU Inc. will have no minimum capital requirement, making it accessible for start-ups and small businesses.
insight_4: EU Inc. aims to simplify cross-border business activities by aiming for a uniform tax framework at the EU level.

file_name: 24 April 2026_Law Against Digital Violence – New Obligations for Online Service Providers.md
paper_title: Law Against Digital Violence – New Obligations for Online Service Providers
insight_1: The draft bill imposes obligations on providers of digital services and internet access services, including new criminal offenses to adapt criminal provisions to the 'new phenomena of the digital age'.
insight_2: Service providers must disclose certain user data upon a court order to victims of specific criminal offenses for civil claims.
insight_3: Providers of internet access services may be required to match and store customer records received from the court.
insight_4: Social network providers may be required to suspend users' accounts who have used their accounts to commit serious infringements, with conditions.

file_name: 26 March 2026_Prediction Markets in Germany and Europe- Legal Status, Risks, and Opportunities.md
paper_title: Prediction Markets in Germany and Europe: Legal Status, Risks, and Opportunities
insight_1: Prediction markets are platforms that allow participants to trade contracts whose value is contingent upon the outcome of a future event.
insight_2: In Germany, prediction markets may be subject to the gambling provisions outlined in the Interstate Treaty on Gambling 2021 (ISTG 2021).
insight_3: Prediction markets can attempt to position themselves as financial derivatives platforms rather than being categorized as gambling services.
insight_4: Operators planning to launch or expand a prediction market platform in Europe must develop a clear and robust regulatory strategy.

file_name: 27 March 2026_EU Inc. – The 28th Regime- How the European Commission intends to revolutionise the formation of companies.md
paper_title: EU Inc. – The 28th Regime: How the European Commission intends to revolutionise the formation of companies
insight_1: The formation of an EU Inc. is intended to be significantly faster, less bureaucratic and more cost-effective compared to previous company formations.
insight_2: An EU Inc. should be capable of being formed with virtually no initial capital according to Article 62(1) of the proposal.
insight_3: The requirement for notarial certification of the transfer of shares is entirely waived in an EU Inc., as a digitally signed contract (in accordance with the eIDAS standard) shall be sufficient.
insight_4: Data submission to various authorities, agencies, registers and social security institutions will be reduced through the use of the European Unique Identifier (EUID).
